scope_note: QA Aliquot Parts Structure cert [328]. Primary source: Iverson, B. (1991) Quantum Arithmetic Volume II -- Books 3 & 4: New Wave Theory / Synchronous Harmonics. Delta Spectrum Research, La Junta CO. pp.13-14 'ALIQUOT PARTS', 'EXAMPLE', 'QUESTIONS'. Five structural results certified: (C1) Primes coprime-to-30 in (0,30) = {7,11,13,17,19,23,29}; with 1 = 8 values; complement pairs sum to 30; 5^2+5=3^3+3=2^5-2=30. (C2) Aliquot candidates from {32,3,5,7,11}: 5 candidates; valid = {3360=32x3x5x7, 5280=32x3x5x11, 7392=32x3x7x11}; invalid = {1155=3x5x7x11 (no power-of-2), 12320=32x5x7x11 (no 3)}; matches Iverson p.14. (C3) Wave gear synchronization: waves X and Y share aliquot A=2x3x5x13x47=18330, unique primes 53 and 7; sync at A*53*7=6800430; X completes 7 cycles, Y completes 53 cycles; general law: after A*p_x*p_y units, X does p_y cycles, Y does p_x cycles. (C4) Three special forms of 30: 5^2+5=30, 3^3+3=30, 2^5-2=30; factored as 5*6, 3*10, 2*15. (C5) Aliquot count = n for n factors; validity requires retaining {power-of-2, 3, and 5-or-7}; from {2,3,5,7}: 2 valid (drop 5 or 7), 2 invalid (drop 2 or 3). Builds on cert [327] (Harmonic Cycle). DOI: primary text, no external DOI.
